description: ZERO significant results after multiple comparison correction. The uncorrected H1b result (p=0.028 at L8) does not survive Bonferroni (p=0.334) or BH-FDR (q=0.107). The paper incorrectly presents uncorrected p-values as evidence of statistical significance.
suggestion: Honestly report zero significant results after correction. Every p-value citation must include MCP context: 'p=0.028 uncorrected; Bonferroni-corrected p=0.334; BH-FDR q=0.107.' Revise framing to acknowledge this is a null-result study.

description: Title-abstract-contributions mismatch: title 'Competitive Suppression in Sparse Autoencoders' and Section 1.4 contribution 'First local inhibition graph for SAE diagnostics' create expectation of a predictive tool. Results show precision@20=0.0 (H6 falsified). Section 5.3 claims 'the graph identifies latents with high total incoming inhibition' despite H8 finding r=+0.12, p=0.55. This is a direct contradiction.
suggestion: If the contribution is the LCA-theoretical framework, retitle to emphasize theory, not graph predictions. Remove 'First local inhibition graph for SAE diagnostics' from contributions. Remove all claims that the graph identifies at-risk features.

description: H9 co-occurrence measurement is a definitional tautology: p_11 + absorption_rate = 1.0 by construction (if parent fires, child is not absorbing; if parent does not fire, child is counted as absorbed). This is a mathematical identity, not an empirical finding. Reported r=-1.0 is a definitional relationship. The paper references H9 in Section 4.8 integration table despite knowing this issue from iteration 1.
suggestion: Remove H9 from the paper entirely. Remove from all tables, integration discussions, and hypothesis summaries.

description: OrtSAE ablation comparison is at unmatched L0 dimensions: OrtSAE without penalty (L0~920) vs OrtSAE with penalty (L0~550). The paper criticizes others for unmatched comparisons but makes the identical mistake. The conclusion 'orthogonality penalty does not appear to reduce absorption' is invalid because the confound explains any difference.
suggestion: Match L0 values for a valid comparison, or explicitly acknowledge this confound invalidates the ablation conclusion.

description: Probe quality confound in CMI-absorption analysis: absorption rate correlates with probe F1 at rho=-0.67 (p<0.001). CMI-absorption analysis uses rates from L0=82 where mean probe F1=0.817. Low-CMI letters may be inherently harder to probe, causing both low estimated CMI and artificially high absorption rates. Paper never computes partial correlation controlling for probe F1.
suggestion: Compute partial correlation controlling for probe F1. Alternatively, restrict CMI analysis to quality-gated letters (F1>0.85).

description: CMI-absorption correlation is dimensionally unstable: rho=-0.383 (p=0.059) at d'=10 reverses sign at d'>=20 (d'=20: rho=+0.048, d'=30: rho=+0.299, d'=50: rho=+0.197). Bonferroni-corrected p=0.236. Sign reversal across dimensions is qualitative failure, not sensitivity issue.
suggestion: Report results across all dimensions. Do not cherry-pick d'=10 as primary. Acknowledge dimension instability as a confound.

description: Post-hoc power analysis (Section 3.6 claims 'approximately 20% power to detect a medium effect size') is methodologically questionable. Power should be computed before the experiment, not after observing null results to explain them away.
suggestion: Remove post-hoc power analysis. If power is low, acknowledge as a design limitation: 'n=26 features provides insufficient power to detect medium effects; null results are inconclusive rather than evidence of no effect.'

description: Feature U (24.2% absorption, 100% steering) is n=1 case evidence overgeneralized to conclude 'absorption is benign.' Other high-absorption features show much lower steering success: H (19.0%) at 0.55, S (16.0%) at 0.65. One feature's behavior does not establish a general principle.
suggestion: Remove the generalization. Report Feature U's result as a single case observation. Do not use it as evidence for general absorption benignity.

description: MCC~0.21 across ALL variants including Random control (MCC=0.197) suggests Hungarian matching yields chance-level recovery regardless of training. The paper acknowledges MCC failure but does not address the implication: absorption reduction may reflect sparsity-induced suppression rather than genuine hierarchical feature recovery.
suggestion: Investigate whether Hungarian matching is valid before claiming any comparisons. If MCC is at chance level for Random SAEs, the entire matching pipeline is suspect.

description: H8 claim (r=+0.12, p=0.55 for total incoming inhibition vs absorption rate) has no verifiable data source. The h6_inhibition_graph.json does not contain per-feature total incoming inhibition statistics. The number appears to be computed but the computation is not documented.
suggestion: Provide reproducible computation or remove specific numbers. Document how total incoming inhibition was computed per feature.
